title: ChemE Agent Eval Fixture
description: A reproducible test case for evaluating AI agents on chemical engineering problems

version.description: Fixture version - increment when the problem or expected answers change

source.description: Where the problem came from - textbook, manual calculation, DWSIM validation

statement.description: Natural language problem description, as you'd read it in a textbook

task.description: What the agent must produce - the specific deliverable

topics.description: ChemE topics exercised, e.g. ['vapor-liquid equilibrium', 'Raoult law', 'flash calculation']

inputs.description: All known values the agent receives. Each value has amount, unit, and optional description.

value.description: Numeric value or array of values

expected_outputs.description: The correct answers. Same structure as inputs.

value.description: Numeric value or array of values

acceptance_criteria.description: How close the agent's answer must be to pass

tolerances.description: Per-output tolerance, keyed by output name

must_include.description: Qualitative checks - things the agent must mention or do, e.g. 'select appropriate thermo model'

must_not_include.description: Red flags that indicate a bad answer, e.g. 'assume ideal gas at high pressure'

domain_context.description: Information about the domain tools and knowledge required

recommended_tools.description: Tools an expert would use, e.g. ['DWSIM', 'Raoult_law_manual_calc']

recommended_thermo_model.description: The appropriate thermodynamic model for this problem

key_assumptions.description: Assumptions a competent engineer would state

common_mistakes.description: Errors students commonly make on this type of problem

agent_evaluation.description: Meta-evaluation: not just was the answer right, but did the agent reason well?

reasoning_checkpoints.description: Key reasoning steps the agent should demonstrate

skill_document_expected.description: Should the agent produce a skill document after solving this?
